embeddings.content:
# Incident Playbook

## Primary Objective

This playbook is used during service degradation, production outage, or high
latency incidents. The goal is to stabilize the affected service, identify the
blast radius, and create clear escalation artifacts for the engineering team.

## First Checks

During an outage, check whether the issue is isolated to production or also
present in staging. Confirm whether the incident is caused by timeout spikes,
retry storms, queue buildup, or a recent configuration rollout. Capture the
first observed timestamp and note whether customer impact is ongoing.

## Escalation Guidance

If a payment-related outage affects transaction authorization or settlement,
open a high severity ticket immediately and page the on-call engineer. If the
issue appears limited to checkout retries without confirmed payment failure,
start with a medium severity triage ticket and collect supporting evidence
before escalating to incident command.

## Supporting Evidence

Useful evidence includes error rates, timeout metrics, retry counts, affected
environments, recent deployment hashes, and links to logs. When a search
workflow is used before ticket creation, the resulting snippets should be
attached to the ticket as supporting context.

## Communication

Use short updates with environment, customer impact, and current mitigation
status. Avoid saying that a service is fully recovered until retry pressure,
error rate, and latency have returned to baseline for at least fifteen
minutes.

## Resolution

After mitigation, update the incident ticket with the root cause, the
mitigation used, and any follow-up tasks. If the same timeout or rerouting
issue has occurred before, link the older incident or workflow run so the team
can compare patterns.
